public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as ward mediator.
appearance: Bright-plumed road gear, polished fittings, and visible attention to presentation mark the role before the voice does.
manner: Moves like someone trained to decide quickly once the threshold for action has been crossed. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a ward mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of ward mediator in and around Guildheart Hub, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Guildheart Hub while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Sena Ledgermere needs help hardening a position, drilling unreliable auxiliaries, or reading an approaching threat around Guildheart Hub.
Sena Ledgermere can pull the party into a dispute over readiness, chain of command, or blamed failure near Guildheart Hub.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V33_1 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Weapon discipline, drilled stillness, and practiced readiness mark Sena Ledgermere as someone tied to organized force rather than casual violence.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Guildheart Hub: drill yards, fortifications, command posts, or exposed routes

first_impression: Sena Ledgermere presents as Moves like someone trained to decide quickly once the threshold for action has been crossed. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a ward mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Ward Mediator, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in The Union.
what_they_refuse: Sena Ledgermere refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Ward Mediator too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Sena Ledgermere can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Sena Ledgermere adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Sena Ledgermere is Known locally through day-to-day work as ward mediator. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Ward Mediator.
social_texture: Sena Ledgermere speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Guildheart Hub, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Sena touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Sena Ledgermere when the party needs a socially grounded face for Guildheart Hub: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
